name: Fresh Veggie Seitan Pita Pocket
intro: Fresh vegetables and tender seitan fill up these pita pockets for a memorable lunch.

ingredients.item: Steamed Seitan Smoky Nuggets, sliced thick (page 283)
baby spinach, chopped, with the long stems cut away and discarded
raw cashews, soaked 1 hour and drained
15-ounce can black beans, drained and rinsed
sprouted grain English muffins, sliced open
Condiments of your choice, such as dairy-free chipotle mayo and horseradish mustard

method: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seitan, broccoli, and bell pepper and cook over medium heat for 3 minutes. Add the cucumber, carrot, and artichoke hearts. Stir and cook for 1 minute. Take off the heat.

Cut the pita pockets in half and spread half the mayonnaise into both halves of each pocket. Add a few leaves of baby spinach and then spoon a quarter of the seitan mixture into each of the four pocket halves.
Add water to a medium saucepan with a steamer insert and bring to a boil. Add the broccoli to the insert and steam over boiling water for 10 minutes. Remove from steamer and set aside.

Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Use a skillet that has a lid. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add the spinach, toss, and cook for 1 minute. Cover and let sit for 2 minutes.
Add the broccoli and spinach to a large bowl.
Add all of the cashew cheese ingredients to a blender and blend until completely smooth.
Pour into a small saucepan and bring heat up to medium high. Keep cooking and stirring until it goes through its thickening process and becomes a gooey, cheesy sauce. This takes 5 to 10 minutes.
Add to the bowl with the broccoli mixture. Stir well.
Cut off the top of the baguette and scoop out some of the bread from the center using a spoon. Make sure you leave an edge of bread in the shell to help hold the filling.
Spoon the broccoli-cheese mixture down the center of the baguette. Top with sliced grape tomatoes and sprinkle with hempseed. Cut in half. You could also cut into slices and serve as an appetizer.

Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the onion and bell pepper and sauté for 10 to 15 minutes or until onion is translucent. Add the garlic, oregano, and cumin and cook for 1 minute. Tear the seiten into chunks and drop into the skillet. Add the black beans. Stir and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes or until seitan is heated through.
Cut each roll in half and scoop out some of the bread center. Spread a layer of salsa on the bread and sprinkle on some chopped romaine lettuce. Fill up with black bean and seitan mixture.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry the patties until just golden on each side.
While the patties are in the skillet, toast the English muffins. Layer the toast with mayo, mustard, avocado, patty, and onion.
